income_thresholds.description: In Low Performing States (LPS), ALL pregnant women delivering in govt facilities are eligible regardless of income. In High Performing States (HPS), only BPL/SC/ST women are eligible.
income_thresholds.source: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare / NHM Guidelines

exclusion_rules.description: Pregnant woman must be at least 19 years of age
In High Performing States, only institutional deliveries (at government or accredited private facilities) qualify. Home deliveries do NOT qualify in HPS.
In High Performing States, cash assistance is restricted to first two live births. In LPS, no birth order restriction for institutional delivery.
In High Performing States (HPS), only BPL, SC, or ST women are eligible

family_criteria.family_definition: Benefit is per pregnancy/delivery to the pregnant woman. In HPS, restricted to first 2 live births. In LPS, no birth order restriction for institutional delivery.

covered_procedures: Cash assistance of Rs 1400 for rural institutional delivery in LPS states
Cash assistance of Rs 1000 for urban institutional delivery in LPS states
Cash assistance of Rs 700 for rural BPL/SC/ST institutional delivery in HPS states
Cash assistance of Rs 600 for urban BPL/SC/ST institutional delivery in HPS states
ASHA incentive of Rs 600 (rural) / Rs 200 (urban) for facilitating delivery
Transport assistance for reaching government health facility
excluded_procedures: Home deliveries in High Performing States
Any medical treatment costs (JSY is a CASH ASSISTANCE scheme, not insurance)
Post-natal treatment beyond the delivery event
Pregnancy complications treatment (covered under JSSK separately)

required_documents.name: BPL Card / SC/ST Certificate
required_documents.local_name_hi: बीपीएल कार्ड / अनुसूचित जाति/जनजाति प्रमाण पत्र
required_documents.notes: Required in High Performing States. In Low Performing States, all pregnant women are eligible regardless.

required_documents.name: MCH Card / ANC Registration
required_documents.notes: Mother and Child Health card with at least 3 ante-natal checkups registered

required_documents.name: Aadhaar Card
required_documents.notes: For identity verification and DBT payment

required_documents.name: Bank Account / Jan Dhan Account
required_documents.notes: Cash assistance transferred directly to mother's bank account via DBT

required_documents.name: Discharge Certificate from Hospital
required_documents.notes: Proof of institutional delivery at government or accredited facility

enrollment_channels: ASHA worker (Accredited Social Health Activist) during pregnancy registration
enrollment_steps: Step 1: Register pregnancy with ASHA worker or at nearest government health facility
Step 2: Get MCH (Mother and Child Health) card with ANC registration
Step 3: Complete at least 3 ante-natal checkups as per schedule
Step 4: ASHA worker assists with institutional delivery planning and transport
Step 5: Deliver at government hospital or accredited private facility
Step 6: Hospital issues discharge certificate with delivery details
Step 7: ASHA worker or hospital submits JSY claim with required documents
Step 8: Cash assistance transferred to mother's bank account within 7 days via DBT
description_for_embedding: Janani Suraksha Yojana (JSY) is a central government cash assistance scheme under the National Health Mission to promote institutional delivery among pregnant women from BPL, SC, and ST families. It provides Rs 1400 (rural) or Rs 1000 (urban) per institutional delivery in Low Performing States, and Rs 700 (rural) or Rs 600 (urban) in High Performing States. The scheme targets reducing maternal and neonatal mortality by incentivizing hospital delivery. Only women aged 19+ are eligible. ASHA workers facilitate registration, transport, and claim processing.
